content.full_text:
Ted 
Date 
2-2-78 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Gina T. 
Advice regarding gifts 
Joan Barton from Senator Song's office called me yesterday and 
asked the following two questions: 
1. 
The Senator received an invitation to go to Disneyland on a 
special "Legislator's Day". 
He received 7 complimentary tickets 
in addition to his own, valued at $11.95 each. 
He wanted to 
know if all 8 tickets were reportable as gifts. 
2. 
20th Century Fox had a special showing of Star Wars for selected 
persons. 
He asked Lee Rosenthal if this was reportable as a 
gift and Lee wrote him back and said it was not because it did 
not reach the $25 threshold. 
However, there was a special dinner 
after the showing and the Senator attended, bringing three other 
persons. 
20th Century Fox paid for all four dinners. 
The 
Senator wanted to know if he must report all four dinners as a 
gift of just his own dinner. 
I asked Ken and he said that Senator Song must report as gifts all 
8 Disneyland Tickets plus all 4 dinners because the extra tickets 
were not specifically given to any of the people he invited. 
cc: 
Ken G. 
Dwight

sections.question: 1. The Senator received an invitation to go to Disneyland on a special "Legislator's Day". He received 7 complimentary tickets in addition to his own, valued at $11.95 each. He wanted to know if all 8 tickets were reportable as gifts. 2. 20th Century Fox had a special showing of Star Wars for selected persons. He asked Lee Rosenthal if this was reportable as a gift and Lee wrote him back and said it was not because it did not reach the $25 threshold. However, there was a special dinner after the showing and the Senator attended, bringing three other persons. 20th Century Fox paid for all four dinners. The Senator wanted to know if he must report all four dinners as a gift of just his own dinner.
sections.conclusion: Senator Song must report as gifts all 8 Disneyland Tickets plus all 4 dinners because the extra tickets were not specifically given to any of the people he invited.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a legislator report complimentary Disneyland tickets received for himself and invitees, and must he report dinners paid for by 20th Century Fox for himself and three guests he brought to a Star Wars screening?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: All eight Disneyland tickets and all four dinners must be reported as gifts. The extra tickets are reportable even though not specifically designated for individual invitees, and all dinners paid by the sponsor must be reported regardless of whether the legislator brought additional guests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal internal FPPC communication. No formal CONCLUSION section header present; conclusion derived from final paragraph. Question section is conversational narrative rather than formal question format. Some ambiguity in whether Ken's statement represents official FPPC position or preliminary guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. The Senator received an invitation to go to Disneyland on a special "Legislator's Day". He received 7 complimentary tickets in addition to his own, valued at $11.95 each. He wanted to know if all 8 tickets were reportable as gifts. 2. 20th Century Fox had a special showing of Star Wars for selected persons. He asked Lee Rosenthal if this was reportable as a gift and Lee wrote him back and said it was not because it did not reach the $25 threshold. However, there was a special dinner after the showing and the Senator attended, bringing three other persons. 20th Century Fox paid for all four dinners. The Senator wanted to know if he must report all four dinners as a gift of just his own dinner.

CONCLUSION: Senator Song must report as gifts all 8 Disneyland Tickets plus all 4 dinners because the extra tickets were not specifically given to any of the people he invited.

However, there was a special dinner after the showing and the Senator attended, bringing three other persons. 20th Century Fox paid for all four dinners. embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter regarding whether a state senator must report complimentary Disneyland tickets and dinners paid by 20th Century Fox as gifts. The FPPC concludes that all eight Disneyland tickets and all four dinners must be reported as gifts.
